task: Write a Python function average(nums) returning the arithmetic mean of the list as a float, even when the mean is a whole number. An empty list returns None, never zero. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function correctly handles the empty list edge case and returns None.
trace: I implemented the arithmetic mean function in Python using the built-in `sum()` and `len()` functions to calculate the total and number of elements in the list. To handle the edge case of a whole number result but never returning zero for an empty list, I checked if the input list is empty and returned None in that case. Lastly, to meet the criterion of providing at least one named edge case, I made sure to explicitly handle the empty list scenario as stated in the task description.
